easy.text: Lena packed a small blue bag for school. She put in a notebook, a pencil, and an apple. At lunch, she shared the apple with her friend Mia.

questions.q: What did Lena share at lunch?

easy.text: Noah planted three seeds in the garden. He watered them every morning. After two weeks, tiny green sprouts appeared.

questions.q: How many seeds did Noah plant?
When did Noah water the seeds?

easy.text: A small turtle crossed the path after the rain. Ava waited quietly until it reached the grass. Then she continued her walk.
The class built paper kites in art time. Ben chose yellow paper and long green ribbon tails. Outside, his kite flew the highest.

questions.q: What color paper did Ben choose?

easy.text: Grandpa told a story by the fireplace. The dog curled at his feet while rain tapped the windows. Everyone listened until the very end.

questions.q: Where did Grandpa tell the story?

easy.text: Milo borrowed a library book about planets. He learned that Mars is called the red planet. At home, he drew Mars in his notebook.

questions.q: What kind of book did Milo borrow?
Which planet is called the red planet?
What did Milo draw at home?

easy.text: At the beach, Sofia collected smooth shells in a bucket. She found one shell with pink stripes and showed it to her brother.

questions.q: What was special about one shell?

easy.text: Ethan fed the chickens before breakfast. One chicken escaped and hid behind the water barrel. Ethan gently guided it back.

questions.q: When did Ethan feed the chickens?
How did Ethan bring it back?

easy.text: The bus arrived early because traffic was light. Nora used the extra time to review spelling words with her friend.

questions.q: Why did the bus arrive early?

easy.text: During music class, Leo tapped a steady beat on a drum. The class clapped in time and sang together.

questions.q: What else did the class do?

medium.text: Maya loved rainy days. She would put on her yellow boots and splash in every puddle she could find. Her mother always had hot chocolate ready when she came inside. It was their special tradition.

questions.q: What did Maya do on rainy days?
What did Maya's mother have ready?

medium.text: The old lighthouse keeper climbed the stairs every evening to light the great lamp. Ships far out at sea depended on that light to find their way safely to the harbor. He never missed a single night in forty years.

questions.q: What did the keeper do every evening?
How long did he keep the light burning?

medium.text: When the power went out during dinner, the family lit candles and continued talking. Without phones or television, they noticed how much they enjoyed the quiet evening together.

questions.q: What did the family use for light?

medium.text: A community team cleaned the riverbank on Saturday morning. They sorted plastic, glass, and paper into separate bags. By noon, the path looked clear and safe again.

questions.q: How did they organize the trash?

options: The path looked clear and safe
questions.answer: The path looked clear and safe

medium.text: Iris practiced free throws every afternoon for two weeks. At first she missed most shots, but she adjusted her stance and focus. In the next game, she scored the winning basket.

questions.q: What did Iris change while practicing?

medium.text: The science club built a small weather station on the school roof. They recorded wind speed, rainfall, and temperature each day. After a month, they found that rainy days were usually cooler.

hard.text: The ancient lighthouse had stood on the cliff for over two hundred years, guiding ships safely through the treacherous waters below. When the government announced plans to replace it with a modern navigation system, the townspeople organized a campaign to save it. They argued that the lighthouse was not just a tool for navigation — it was a symbol of their community's history and resilience.

questions.q: How long had the lighthouse been standing?
Why did the government want to replace it?

options: Organized a campaign to save it
questions.answer: Organized a campaign to save it

questions.q: What can you infer about the townspeople?

hard.text: Although the city planned to replace its historic tramline with buses, residents argued that the tram connected neighborhoods in ways that improved both commerce and community identity. A pilot study later showed that areas near tram stops had higher foot traffic for local shops.

questions.q: Why did residents oppose replacing the tramline?
options: The tram supported commerce and identity
The tram was faster than trains
questions.answer: The tram supported commerce and identity

questions.q: What did the pilot study measure?

options: Transport has no effect on neighborhoods
The tram may help local businesses
questions.answer: The tram may help local businesses

questions.q: Which word best describes the residents' argument?

hard.text: A startup introduced reusable shipping containers that could be folded and returned after delivery. Initial costs were higher than disposable packaging, but long-term analysis showed reduced waste fees and lower material spending after six months.

questions.q: What innovation did the startup introduce?

options: Waste fees and material spending dropped
questions.answer: Waste fees and material spending dropped

questions.q: What is the central trade-off described?
options: Higher upfront cost vs long-term savings
questions.answer: Higher upfront cost vs long-term savings

hard.text: A municipal report examined waste management after introducing reusable container system. Analysts compared baseline figures with quarterly data on collection volume and replacement cost, while controlling for seasonal variation. Although implementation required additional training, the findings suggested that upfront investment produced long-term savings.

questions.q: What intervention did the report analyze?
What is the strongest supported inference?
Which phrase best characterizes the report's method?

hard.text: A municipal report examined energy planning after introducing smart lighting controls. Analysts compared baseline figures with quarterly data on consumption peaks and maintenance logs, while controlling for seasonal variation. Although implementation required additional training, the findings suggested that data-informed scheduling lowered operating costs.

questions.q: What intervention did the report analyze?
What is the strongest supported inference?
Which phrase best characterizes the report's method?

hard.text: A municipal report examined local economy after introducing weekend street market. Analysts compared baseline figures with quarterly data on foot traffic and small-business sales, while controlling for seasonal variation. Although implementation required additional training, the findings suggested that accessible events supported local vendors.

questions.q: What intervention did the report analyze?
What is the strongest supported inference?
Which phrase best characterizes the report's method?

hard.text: A municipal report examined public transit after introducing protected bike lanes. Analysts compared baseline figures with quarterly data on modal split and commute time, while controlling for seasonal variation. Although implementation required additional training, the findings suggested that multimodal planning can reduce congestion.

questions.q: What intervention did the report analyze?
What is the strongest supported inference?
Which phrase best characterizes the report's method?

hard.text: A municipal report examined school policy after introducing extended project blocks. Analysts compared baseline figures with quarterly data on completion rates and revision quality, while controlling for seasonal variation. Although implementation required additional training, the findings suggested that longer focused time improved complex work.

questions.q: What intervention did the report analyze?
What is the strongest supported inference?
options: longer focused time improved complex work
questions.answer: longer focused time improved complex work

questions.q: Which phrase best characterizes the report's method?

hard.text: A municipal report examined library services after introducing digital lending program. Analysts compared baseline figures with quarterly data on wait times and circulation rates, while controlling for seasonal variation. Although implementation required additional training, the findings suggested that flexible access increased participation.

questions.q: What intervention did the report analyze?
What is the strongest supported inference?
Which phrase best characterizes the report's method?

hard.text: A regional task force evaluated local economy after introducing weekend street market using anonymized administrative data. The team compared baseline benchmarks with quarterly indicators for foot traffic and small-business sales, and adjusted models for seasonal effects. Despite short-term implementation friction, the evidence indicated that accessible events supported local vendors.

questions.q: Which intervention is central to the evaluation?
Which conclusion is best supported by the passage?
Why mention seasonal effects in the analysis?

easy.text: Ο μεγάλος σκύλος έτρεξε στο πάρκο. Έπαιξε με μια μπάλα. Μετά ήπιε λίγο νερό.
Η Μαρία έχει μια γάτα. Η γάτα είναι μαύρη και άσπρη. Της αρέσει να κοιμάται στο κρεβάτι.

medium.text: Η Ελένη αγαπούσε τις βροχερές μέρες. Φορούσε τις κίτρινες γαλότσες της και πηδούσε σε κάθε λακκούβα. Η μαμά της είχε πάντα ζεστή σοκολάτα έτοιμη όταν γύριζε σπίτι.

questions.q: Τι έκανε η Ελένη στη βροχή;

medium.text: Ο Νίκος έφτιαξε ένα μικρό θερμοκήπιο στο μπαλκόνι. Κάθε απόγευμα μετρούσε τη θερμοκρασία και πότιζε τα φυτά. Σε έναν μήνα, οι ντομάτες μεγάλωσαν γρήγορα.

hard.text: Ο παλιός φάρος στεκόταν στον βράχο για πάνω από διακόσια χρόνια, οδηγώντας τα πλοία με ασφάλεια μέσα από τα επικίνδυνα νερά. Όταν η κυβέρνηση ανακοίνωσε σχέδια να τον αντικαταστήσει με σύγχρονο σύστημα πλοήγησης, οι κάτοικοι οργάνωσαν εκστρατεία για να τον σώσουν.

questions.q: Γιατί ήθελε η κυβέρνηση να τον αντικαταστήσει;

hard.text: Η πόλη αποφάσισε να δημιουργήσει περισσότερους ποδηλατόδρομους για να μειώσει την κίνηση και τη ρύπανση. Τους πρώτους μήνες υπήρχαν αντιδράσεις, όμως τα νέα στοιχεία έδειξαν καλύτερη μετακίνηση στο κέντρο και χαμηλότερα επίπεδα θορύβου.
